title: Designing brand intelligence: from rules to expression
key_points: Argues that in an AI-powered design world the designer's edge is shifting away from technical execution toward how you think, decide, and design when change outpaces your design system.

Reimagines brand guides as cognitive frameworks rather than rigid rule sets, drawing inspiration from J Dilla, who broke away from rigid quantization in rhythm to create work that felt more human, fluid, and felt.
Offers practical guidance on organizing brands by cognition and building interactive brand frameworks directly in Figma.
Frames an evolution in the designer's role from hands-on makers to strategic directors.
